content: rn its c#. honestly i can do every language except java. i will never touch it
Just saw brown dust and azure Lane marketing video
I think somebody's spying on me
my entire coding project is java...
i love java. java is a friend to me. i am used to it
c is confusing because it's similar to java but *just* different enough it always trips me up
do not talk to me about coding in risc-v assembly. do not code in assembly. it isn't worth it
(had to do assembly in one of my classes this semester. *do not code in assembly it isn't worth it*)
fym if u doing java then one day u will have to touch c
when its easy enough its fun
when it's stuff like adding or multiplying it isn't too bad
yeah, till them bringing the classic 2d array i just shitted my pants
but then you get to loading and storing memory in registers like A[i] or stuff like that, or trying to use ecall, and then it becomes a nightmare
my last semester test was that thing
arrays with assembly are uhhhhh Not Easy! not easy at all
yeah, before our last test and the final for the class had the grade curve applied everybody made like, a 30 or something out of 100 or things like that lol
i got the final for this class and the last test curved and my grade jumped from a 74 to a 90 bc everybody did horribly on the assembly parts of it 😭
life tip if you don't have to take assembly for whatever major or minor you're going for in college don't take assembly unless you're prepared to suffer
too bad its infosec, assembly is a must gtta say....
i'm lucky my compsci major is bioinformatics focused bc i get to poke at the bio half of compsci stuff (. . . eventually)
till then i suffer with linear algebra because i like math but it sure don't like me
whoa thats is the first time i heard about it
you guys really do use matrices in bio research thov
i had to withdraw from my linear algebra class this semester bc my professor did Not teach well so i'm hoping this time it'll be better <:flop1:1356083604236800030>

eeeh, kinda? i mean thre's like, waterman-smith matrices or the like, BLOSUM matrices that are used for traceback of DNA sequences and stuff, but i don't know as they're necessarily similar to the kind of matrices one uses in linear algebra
anyways bioinformatics is a neat little intersection of computer science, biology, throw in some mathematics and chemistry and whatnot, it's a fun thing

very funny seeing people approach bioinformatics from a compsci angle vs a bio angle because it's interesting seeing what aspects they're not used to or struggle more with learning depending on which direction they're coming from
i kept up w/my bio classes at my community college while doing compsci stuff so it's very funny

its the same stuff, teachers and the education talking in general do not express how does matrices relating to the subject too well. if u learning linear algebra its just teaching you how to use matrices
i see! hopefully i'll have an easier time learning to use those then, since i've got some more experience from my one bioinformatics class
lucky for me i dont have to touch it ever<:tuff_ramona:1441426630420467753>
also *yeah* never go to assembly. do not pass go do not collect $200 do not do assembly
something something we all hate assembly
not really we infosec really like assembly
there's tool using to translate assembly into languages thats easier to understand
:0 really??? man, a resource like that would have been *so* useful these past few weeks
it only for viruses, malwares analytic's edge case usage, we do use it just not everytime
and i doubt it will help you lol
you're a psycho for having all your nails being different lengths
My science teacher's nail specifically the thumb is like this while everything else is short <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>
luma the noob and Niko \(lorekeeper i guess\) were playing
